name: Spaghetti Aglio e Olio
tagline: Garlic, olive oil, chili — the four-ingredient Roman classic
highlight: Four ingredients, twenty minutes, peak Italian

hero.alt: Twirl of spaghetti glossy with garlic oil, chili flakes, and parsley
hero.caption: aglio e olio — garlic, oil, chili

healthFacts: No nuts, no dairy — naturally nut-free, vegan-friendly.
Garlic's allicin forms when raw garlic is crushed; gentle warming preserves more than searing.
Extra-virgin olive oil's polyphenols are stable up to about 374°F (190°C).

steps.title: Boil heavily salted water
steps.detail: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Salt it like the sea — pasta absorbs about 1 tsp of salt per portion. Drop spaghetti and cook 1 minute under package time.
steps.tip: Reserve a cup of starchy pasta water before draining; it's the emulsifier.

steps.title: Slowly bloom the garlic
steps.detail: While pasta cooks, warm olive oil in a wide pan over medium-low. Add sliced garlic and chili flakes. Cook gently 4–5 minutes until garlic turns pale gold — not brown.
steps.tip: Brown garlic is bitter garlic. Pull it off heat the moment it turns straw-yellow.

media.alt: Sliced garlic and chili sizzling in olive oil
media.caption: bloom the garlic

steps.title: Emulsify with pasta water
steps.detail: Add 1/2 cup pasta water to the garlic oil and bring to a simmer — it'll emulsify into a glossy sauce. Tongs the pasta directly in.
steps.tip: Sauce too thin? Add another splash of pasta water and toss vigorously.

media.alt: Pasta water emulsifying with garlic oil
media.caption: emulsify with pasta water

steps.title: Toss and serve
steps.detail: Toss vigorously over heat 1 minute so the pasta finishes cooking in the sauce and absorbs flavor. Off heat, fold in chopped parsley. Plate immediately.
steps.tip: Aglio e olio waits for no one — it tightens up fast on the plate.
